data.narrative:
Newcleo is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Stefano Buono, Luciano Cinotti, and Elisabeth Rizzotti, having raised €537M to date. The valuation universe consists of 8 private peers in nuclear energy—including Type One Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power—alongside 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR).

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage peer density. While 8 same-sector private companies exist, fewer than three datapoints qualify when filtered by identical or adjacent stage and comparable valuation tier. Broadening to all-sector medians would introduce material heterogeneity in technology maturity, capital intensity, and regulatory risk profiles, rendering cross-sector multiples unreliable for this specialized frontier-tech domain.

The lack of granular triangulation means valuation depends heavily on qualitative assessment of Newcleo's competitive position within the nuclear cohort, capital requirements relative to peers' burn profiles, and inflection points in regulatory or commercialization timelines. Key uncertainties include the degree to which public comps' valuations reflect near-term deployment risk versus long-term optionality, heterogeneity in technology readiness levels across private peers, and sector-wide sensitivity to policy and interest-rate shifts. A credible valuation range would require structured scenario modeling around commercialization probability and time-to-revenue, anchored to the capital efficiency demonstrated by top-quartile peers in this cohort.
